summary.para: A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to control if dynamic batching is enabled.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the view distance of decals.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the application update rate using Application.targetFrameRate.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for when the quality level is increased/scaler level decreased.
Callback for when the quality level is decreased/scaler level increased.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer for adjusting https:docs.unity3d.comScriptReferenceCamera-layerCullDistances.html|layer culling distances.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer for adjusting at what distance LODs are switched.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the size of the palette used for color grading in URP.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the level of antialiasing.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.

This attribute is used to tag classes as providing build settings support for an Adaptive Performance provider. The unified setting system will present the settings as an inspectable object in the Project Settings window using the built-in inspector UI.
The implementor of the settings is able to create their own custom UI and the Project Settings system will use that UI in place of the build-in one in the Inspector. See the https:docs.unity3d.comManualExtendingTheEditor.html|Extending the Editor page in the Unity Manual for more information.
The key used to store the singleton instance of these settings within EditorBuildSettings.
The display name that the user sees in the Project Settings window.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceConfigurationDataAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)

summary.para: Constructor for attribute

member.param: The display name to use in the Project Settings window.
The key to use to get or set build settings with.

summary.para: A ScriptableObject that contains global settings applicable to all Adaptive Performance providers.
The key used to query to get the current loader settings.
The current active manager used to manage the Adaptive Performance lifetime.
Used to set if the manager is activated and initialized on startup.
The current settings instance.
Indicates if provider loader has been initialized.
Indicates if provider loader and subsystem has been started.
The current active manager used to manage the Adaptive Performance lifetime.

Higher level implementation of Adaptive performance that tracks performance and thermal states of the device and provides them to AdaptivePerformanceScaler which use the information to increase or decrease performance levels. System acts as AdaptivePerformanceScaler manager and handles the lifetime of the scalers in the scenes.
Returns Time.deltaTime only and is primarily encapsulated for tests.

returns.para: delta time

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceIndexer.GetAllRegisteredScalers(System.Collections.Generic.List`1<UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceScaler>&)

summary.para: Returns all scalers independent of their state.

member.param: Output where scalers will be written.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceIndexer.GetAppliedScalers(System.Collections.Generic.List`1<UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceScaler>&)

summary.para: Returns all currently applied scalers.

member.param: Output where scalers will be written.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceIndexer.GetDisabledScalers(System.Collections.Generic.List`1<UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceScaler>&)

summary.para: Returns all currently disabled scalers.

member.param: Output where scalers will be written.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceIndexer.GetUnappliedScalers(System.Collections.Generic.List`1<UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceScaler>&)

summary.para: Returns all currently unapplied scalers.

member.param: Output where scalers will be written.

summary.para: Current determined action needed from performance state. Action StateAction.Increase will be ignored if ThermalAction is decreasing.
Current determined action needed from thermal state. Action StateAction.Increase will be ignored if PerformanceAction is decreasing.
Time left until next action.
Unapply all currently active scalers.
Settings of indexer system.
Returns true if Indexer was active, false otherwise.
Delay after any scaler is applied or unapplied because of performance state.
Delay after any scaler is applied or unapplied because of thermal state.

Adaptive Performance Loader abstract class used as a base class for specific provider implementations. Providers should implement subclasses of this to provide specific initialization and management implementations that make sense for their supported scenarios and needs.
Ask loader to deinitialize all initialized subsystems.

returns.para: Whether or not deinitialization succeeded.

summary.para: Gets the loaded default subsystem.

returns.para: The loaded subsystem, or null if no default subsystem is loaded.

summary.para: Gets the loaded subsystem of the specified type. This is implementation-specific, because implementations contain data on what they have loaded and how best to get it.

returns.para: The loaded subsystem, or null if no subsystem found.

summary.para: Gets the Settings of the loader used to descibe the loader and subsystems.

returns.para: The settings of the loader.

summary.para: Initialize the loader. This should initialize all subsystems to support the desired runtime setup this loader represents.

returns.para: Whether or not initialization succeeded.

summary.para: Returns if the provider loader was initialized successfully.
Returns if the provider loader is currently running.
Ask loader to start all initialized subsystems.

returns.para: Whether or not all subsystems were successfully started.

summary.para: Ask loader to stop all initialized subsystems.

returns.para: Whether or not all subsystems were successfully stopped.

summary.para: Adaptive Performance Loader abstract subclass used as a base class for specific provider implementations. Class provides some helper logic that can be used to handle subsystem handling in a typesafe manner, reducing potential boilerplate code.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceLoaderHelper.CreateSubsystem(System.Collections.Generic.List`1<TDescriptor>,System.String)

summary.para: Creates a subsystem with a given list of descriptors and a specific subsystem id.

member.param: List of TDescriptor instances to use for subsystem matching.
The identifier key of the particualr subsystem implementation being requested.

summary.para:
Override of Deinitialize to provide for clearing the instance map.
If you override Deinitialize in your subclass, you must call the base implementation to allow the instance map tp be cleaned up correctly.

returns.para: True if de-initialization was successful.

summary.para: Destroy a subsystem instance of a given type. Subsystem is assumed to already be loaded from a previous call to CreateSubsystem.
Gets the loaded subsystem of the specified type. This is implementation-specific, because implementations contain data on what they have loaded and how best to get it.

returns.para: The loaded subsystem, or null if no subsystem found.

summary.para: Start a subsystem instance of a given type. Subsystem is assumed to already be loaded from a previous call to CreateSubsystem.
Stop a subsystem instance of a given type. Subsystem is assumed to already be loaded from a previous call to CreateSubsystem.

Class to handle active loader and subsystem management for Adaptive Performance. This class is to be added as a ScriptableObject asset in your project and should only be referenced by an AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettings instance for its use.
Given a list of loaders, it will attempt to load each loader in the given order. Unity will use the first loader that is successful and ignore all remaining loaders. The successful loader is accessible through the activeLoader property on the manager.
Depending on configuration, the AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettings instance will automatically manage the active loader at the correct points in the application lifecycle. You can override certain points in the active loader lifecycle and manually manage them by toggling the AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings.automaticLoading and AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings.automaticRunning properties. Disabling AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings.automaticLoading implies that you are responsible for the full lifecycle of the Adaptive Performance session normally handled by the AdaptivePerformanceGeneralSettings instance. Setting this to false also sets AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings.automaticRunning to false.
Disabling AdaptivePerformanceManagerSettings.automaticRunning only implies that you are responsible for starting and stopping the activeLoader through the StartSubsystems and StopSubsystems APIs.
Unity executes atomatic lifecycle management as follows:
" OnEnable calls InitializeLoader internally. The loader list will be iterated over and the first successful loader will be set as the active loader. " Start calls StartSubsystems internally. Ask the active loader to start all subsystems. " OnDisable calls StopSubsystems internally. Ask the active loader to stop all subsystems. " OnDestroy calls DeinitializeLoader internally. Deinitialize and remove the active loader.
Returns the current singleton active loader instance.

Get and set Automatic Loading state for this manager. When this is true, the manager will automatically call InitializeLoader and DeinitializeLoader for you. When false, automaticRunning is also set to false and remains that way. This means that disabling automatic loading disables all automatic behavior for the manager.

Get and set the automatic running state for this manager. When this is true, the manager will call StartSubsystems and StopSubsystems APIs at appropriate times. When false, or when automaticLoading is false, it is up to the user of the manager to handle that same functionality.

Read-only boolean that is true if initialization is completed and false otherwise. Because initialization is handled as a Coroutine, applications that use the auto-lifecycle management of AdaptivePerformanceManager will need to wait for init to complete before checking for an ActiveLoader and calling StartSubsystems.
List of loaders currently managed by this Adaptive Performance Manager instance.
Returns the current active loader, cast to the requested type. Useful shortcut when you need to get the active loader as something less generic than AdaptivePerformanceLoader.

returns.para: The active loader as requested type, or null if no active loader currently exists.

summary.para: Profiler Stats reporting helper class. Stores all adaptive performance markers and helper functions.
GUID for the Adaptive Performance Profile Module definition.
The Scaler data tag defines a tag for the scalers to send them via the emit frame data function.
Profiler Category is set to scripts for Adaptive Performance.
Profiler counter to report cpu average frametime.
Profiler counter to report average frametime.
Profiler counter to report gpu average frametime.
Profiler counter to report the bottleneck.
Profiler counter to report cpu performance level.
Profiler counter to report cpu frametime.
Profiler counter to report frametime.
Profiler counter to report gpu performance level.
Profiler counter to report gpu frametime.
Custom Profiler Marker that allows you to sample different data from Unity Profiler.
Construct the marker with the name of the marker and the dataUnit the marker is collected on.
Sample a single data point in the frame.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceProfilerStats.EmitScalerDataToProfilerStream(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Single,System.Boolean,System.Int32)

summary.para: Adaptive Performance sends scaler data to the profiler each frame. It is collected from multiple places with this method and flushed once with FlushScalerDataToProfilerStream.

member.param: The name of the scaler. 320 characters max.
If the scaler is currently enabled.
The override state of the scaler.
The current level of the scaler.
The actual scale of the scaler.
If the scaler is currently applied.
The maximum level of the scaler.

summary.para: Flushes the Adaptive Performance scaler data for this frame. Used in conjunction with EmitScalerDataToProfilerStream.
Profiler counter to report the performance mode.
ScalerInfo is a struct used to collect and send scaler info to the profile collectively.
State if the scaler is currently applied.
The current level of the scaler.
If the scaler is currently enabled.
The maximum level of the scaler.
The override state of the scaler.
The actual scale of the scaler.
The name of the scaler. 320 characters max.
Profiler counter to report the temperature level.
Profiler counter to report the temperature trend.
Profiler counter to report the thermal warning level.
This class is used to store changes to a number of rendering quality settings that are applied when using the Universal Render Pipeline.
Adjust the quality of MSAA.
Adjust the drawdistance for decals.
Adjust the size of lookup tables that are used for color grading.
Adjust the number of shadow cascades for the main camera in the scene.
Amount to multiply the main lights shadowmap resolution. Values are clamped between 0 and 1.
Adjust how far in the distance shadows will be rendered. Values are clamped between 0 and 1.
Lower the resolution of the main camera to reduce fillrate and GPU load.
Adjust the quality setting of shadows.
Whether dynamic batching should be used when rendering multiple objects that share the same material. Useful on hardware that does not support instancing.
Whether depth-based sorting should be enabled. When enabled, there is a higher load on the CPU but less rendering overdraw. When disabled, there is less CPU pressure but more overdraw.
Whether transparent objects should be rendered When enabled, there is less rendering overdraw, but entire objects can disappear.

Abstract class representing single feature that is controlled by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer. You control the quality through changing the levels, where 0 represents the controller not being applied and 1,2... as applied. As a result, a higher level represents lower visuals, but better performance.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceScaler.ApplyDefaultSetting(UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceScalerSettingsBase)

summary.para: Any scaler with settings in IAdaptivePerformanceSettings needs to call this method and provide the scaler specific setting. Unity uses the setting arguments in the base-scaler as the default settings. This is also used by Scaler Profiles to apply their Settings.

member.param: The settings to apply to the scaler.

summary.para: Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Calculate the cost of applying this particular scaler.

returns.para: Cost of scaler ranges from 0 to infinity.

summary.para: Scaler impact on CPU so far in milliseconds.
Returns the current level of scale.
Default settings for this scaler.
Returns true if this scaler is active, otherwise returns false.
Scaler impact on GPU so far in milliseconds.
Returns true if the scaler can no longer be applied, otherwise returns false.
Returns the maximum boundary of this scaler.
Returns the maximum level of this scaler.
Returns the minimum boundary of this scaler.
Returns a string with the name of the scaler.
Returns true if the scaler is not applied, otherwise returns false.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change
Callback for when the performance level is decreased.
Callback for when the performance level is increased.
Allows you to manually override the scaler level. If the value is -1, AdaptivePerformanceIndexer will handle levels, otherwise scaler will be forced to the value you specify.
Returns a generic scale of this scaler in range [0,1] which is applied to the quality.
Checks if scale changed based on the current level and returns true if scale changed false otherwise.

returns.para: Returns true if scale changed false otherwise.

summary.para: Returns the bottlenecks that this scaler targets.
Returns the visual impact of scaler when applied.
Scaler profiles are used to combine all settings of scalers into one profile to be able to change the settings of each scaler at once.
List of custom scalers added to the provider settings via the scaler profile UI.
Name of the Scaler Profile. Used to find profiles and switch them during runtime.
Settings of indexer system.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to control if dynamic batching is enabled.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to change decal properties.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the application update rate using Application.targetFrameRate.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to change the layer culling distance.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer for adjusting at what distance LODs are switched.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the size of the palette used for color grading in URP.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the level of antialiasing.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to change physics properties.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the resolution of all render targets that allow dynamic resolution.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the number of shadow cascades to be used.
Obsolete: Please use AdaptiveShadowCascade.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to change the distance at which shadows are rendered.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the resolution of shadow maps.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the quality of shadows.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to change if objects in the scene are sorted by depth before rendering to reduce overdraw.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to disable transparent objects rendering.
A scaler setting used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to change the view distance.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceScalerSettings.ApplySettings(UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.AdaptivePerformanceScalerSettings)

summary.para: Apply existing external settings to a scaler to override the existing settings.

member.param: Provide existing settings to replace the default settings.

summary.para: Returns the list of default scaler settings.
Settings of indexer system.
Returns true if Indexer was active, false otherwise.
Maximum value for the scale boundary.
Maximum level for the scaler. This is tied to the implementation of the scaler to divide the levels into concrete steps.
Minimum value for the scale boundary.
Returns the name of the scaler.
Scale to control the quality impact for the scaler. No quality change when 1, improved quality when bigger 1, and lowered quality when smaller 1.
Application bottleneck that the scaler targets. The target selected has the most impact on the quality control of this scaler.
Visual impact the scaler has on the application. The higher the value, the more impact the scaler has on the visuals.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer for adjusting how physics is applied.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.

A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the resolution of all render targets that allow dynamic resolution. If a device or graphics API doesn't support a dynamic resolution, it will use the rendering pipeline's render scale multiplier as a fallback.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the number of shadow cascades to be used.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to change the distance at which shadows are rendered.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the resolution of shadow maps.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to adjust the quality of shadows.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to change if objects in the scene are sorted by depth before rendering to reduce overdraw.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer to toggle rendering of transparent objects.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback for any level change
A scaler used by AdaptivePerformanceIndexer for adjusting what view distance is applied to the camera.
Ensures settings are applied during startup.
Callback when scaler gets disabled and removed from indexer
Callback when scaler gets enabled and added to the indexer
Callback for any level change.
Static class that stores the setting key for basic provider.
unique setting key for basic provider that is used to save/retrieve the assets in the editor.
Basic provider Settings.
The cluster info describes the CPU Cluster setup.
Number of big cores supported by the CPU.
Number of little cores supported by the CPU.
Number of medium cores supported by the CPU.
Constants used by Adaptive Performance.
The number of past frames that are considered to calculate average frame times.
The maximum temperature level. See ThermalMetrics.TemperatureLevel.
The minimum CPU level. Used by IDevicePerformanceControl.CpuLevel and PerformanceMetrics.CurrentCpuLevel.
The minimum GPU level. Used by IDevicePerformanceControl.GpuLevel and PerformanceMetrics.CurrentGpuLevel.
The minimum temperature level. See ThermalMetrics.TemperatureLevel.

UnknownPerformanceLevel is the value of IDevicePerformanceControl.GpuLevel, PerformanceMetrics.CurrentGpuLevel, IDevicePerformanceControl.CpuLevel, and PerformanceMetrics.CurrentCpuLevel if the current performance level is unknown. This can happen when AdaptivePerformance is not supported or when the device is in throttling state (see WarningLevel.Throttling).
FrameTiming stores timing information about CPU, GPU, and the overall frame time.

Returns the main thread CPU time as an average over the past 100 frames (in seconds). Returns -1.0f if this is not available (for example, in the first frame or directly after resume). The CPU time includes only the time the CPU spent executing Unity's main and/or render threads.
The overall frame time as an average over the past 100 frames (in seconds). Returns -1.0f if no timing information is available (for example, in the first frame or directly after resume).

Returns the overall frame time as an average over the past 100 frames (in seconds). Returns -1.0f if no timing information is available. The GPU time only includes the time the GPU spent on rendering a frame (for example, in the first frame or directly after resume).

Returns the main thread CPU time of the last frame (in seconds). The CPU time includes only time the CPU spent executing Unity's main and/or render threads. Returns -1.0f if no timing information is available (for example, in the first frame or directly after resume).
The overall frame time in seconds. Returns -1.0f if no timing information is available (for example, in the first frame or directly after resume).

Returns the GPU time of the last completely rendered frame (in seconds). Returns -1.0f if no timing information is available. The GPU time only includes the time the GPU spent on rendering a frame (for example, in the first frame or directly after resume).
Global access to the default Adaptive Performance interface and lifecycle management controls.
Stops Adaptive Performance (if still running) and initiates the tear down process. Once complete, Instance is no longer available. All Adaptive Performance objects are removed from the scene.

Create and attach the Adaptive Performance to the scene and initiate the startup process for the provider. After initialization is complete, Instance is made available.
Should only be used when "Initialize on Startup" is disabled.
Global access to the default Adaptive Performance interface to access the main manager object.
Subscribe to Adaptive Performance lifecycle events which are sent when the Instance value changes.
The main interface to access Adaptive Performance. None of the properties in this interface change after startup. This means the references returned by the properties may be cached by the user.
Returns true if Adaptive Performance was initialized and is actively running, false otherwise. This means that Adaptive Performance is enabled in StartupSettings.
Stops Adaptive Performance (if still running) and initiates the tear down process. When this completes successfully, Initialized will be false.
Access to development (logging) settings.
Control CPU and GPU performance of the device.
Access to the Indexer system. See AdaptivePerformanceIndexer

Initiates the initialization process for Adaptive Performance by attempting to initialize the loaders. When this completes successfully, Initialized will be true. Adaptive Performance can now be started by calling the StartAdaptivePerformance method.

Returns true if Adaptive Performance was initialized successfully, false otherwise. This means that Adaptive Performance is enabled in StartupSettings and the application runs on a device that supports Adaptive Performance.
Access performance mode status information of the device.
Access performance status information of the device and your application.
Access to the Settings. See IAdaptivePerformanceSettings.
Attempts to start Adaptive Performance by requesting the active loader and all subsystems to start. When this completes successfully, Active will be true.
Attempts to stop Adaptive Performance by requesting the active loader and all subsystems to stop. When this completes successfully, Active will be false.
Access to the active Subsystem. See Provider.AdaptivePerformanceSubsystem.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.IAdaptivePerformance.SupportedFeature(UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.Provider.Feature)

summary.para: List of supported Features by the loaded provider. See Provider.Feature.

member.param: The feature in question. See Provider.Feature.

returns.para: True if the requested feature is supported, false otherwise.

summary.para: Access thermal status information of the device.
Provider Settings Interface as base class of the provider. Used to control the Editor runtime asset instance which stores the Settings.
Whether automated target frame rate based on device GameMode settings should be used.
The initial value of IDevicePerformanceControl.AutomaticPerformanceControl.
Default scaler profile index.
Whether CPU and GPU boost mode should be enabled on application startup.
Settings of indexer system.

Control debug logging. This setting only affects development builds. All logging is disabled in release builds. This setting can also be controlled after startup using IDevelopmentSettings.Logging. Logging is disabled by default.
Settings of scaler system.

Adjust the frequency in frames at which the application logs frame statistics to the console. This is only relevant when logging is enabled. See IDevelopmentSettings.Logging. This setting can also be controlled after startup using IDevelopmentSettings.LoggingFrequencyInFrames.
The currently active scaler profile. Only one scaler profile is active at a time.
Add a new scaler profile with default scaler settings.
Delete a scaler profile at the given index.
Returns a list of all available scaler profiles.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.IAdaptivePerformanceSettings.LoadScalerProfile(System.String)

summary.para: Load a scaler profile from the settings. Unity update the values of all scalers in the profile to new ones. This is a heavy operation using reflection and should not be used per frame and only in load operations as it causes hitching and possible screen artifacts depending on which scalers are used in a scene.

member.param: Supply the name of the scaler. You can query a list of available scaler profiles via IAdaptivePerformanceSettings.GetAvailableScalerProfiles.

summary.para: When Unity enables the serialized object it upgrades old files to the new format in the editor and saves the assets. Empty during runtime.
List of created scaler profiles for this provider.
Use the developer settings interface to access and change settings which are available only in development mode.
Returns true if logging was enabled in StartupSettings.
Adjust the frequency in frames at which the application logs frame statistics to the console. This is only relevant when logging is enabled.

The device performance control interface handles all control elements related to the device performance. You can change the IDevicePerformanceControl.AutomaticPerformanceControl settings or retrieve information about the CpuLevel and GpuLevel.
When set to true, which is the default value, Adaptive Performance automatically sets CpuLevel and GpuLevel.

The requested CPU performance level. Higher levels typically allow CPU cores to run at higher clock speeds. The consequence is that thermal warnings and throttling might happen sooner when the device cannot sustain high clock speeds. Changes are applied once per frame. It is recommended to set the CpuLevel as low as possible to save power. The valid value range is [Constants.MinCpuPerformanceLevel, IDevicePerformanceControl.MaxCpuPerformanceLevel].

The requested CPU boost mode state. Enabled typically allows CPU cores to run at higher clock speeds. The consequence is that thermal warnings and throttling might happen sooner when the device cannot sustain high clock speeds. Changes are applied once per frame. It is recommended to not use a boost often and certainly not continuously to save power.

The requested GPU performance level. Higher levels typically allow the GPU to run at higher clock speeds. The consequence is that thermal warnings and throttling might happen sooner when the device cannot sustain high clock speeds. Changes are applied once per frame. It is recommended to set the GpuLevel as low as possible to save power. The valid value range is [Constants.MinGpuPerformanceLevel, IDevicePerformanceControl.MaxGpuPerformanceLevel].

The requested GPU boost mode state. Enabled typically allows GPU cores to run at higher clock speeds. The consequence is that thermal warnings and throttling might happen sooner when the device cannot sustain high clock speeds. Changes are applied once per frame. It is recommended to not use a boost often and certainly not continuously to save power.
The maximum valid CPU performance level you use with CpuLevel. The minimum value returned is Constants.MinCpuPerformanceLevel. This value does not change after startup is complete.
The maximum valid GPU performance level you use with GpuLevel. The minimum value returned is Constants.MinGpuPerformanceLevel. This value does not change after startup is complete.
The current PerformanceControlMode. PerformanceControlMode is affected by AutomaticPerformanceControl.
Use the performance mode status interface to receive performance mode status events of the device.
The latest performance mode available.
Subscribe to performance mode events which Adaptive Performance sends when the performance mode of the device changes.
You can use the performance status interface to obtain performance metrics, frame timing, and subscribe to bottleneck and performance event changes.
Allows you to query the latest frame timing measurements.
Subscribe to events and get updates when the the current CPU or GPU is boosted.
Subscribe to performance events and get updates when the bottleneck changes.
Subscribe to events and get updates when the the current CPU or GPU level changes.
Allows you to query the latest performance metrics.
Allows you to query the latest performance mode.
Use the thermal status interface to receive thermal status events and thermal metrics of the device.
Subscribe to thermal events which Adaptive Performance sends when the thermal state of the device changes.
The latest thermal metrics available.
Types of Adaptive Performance lifecycle changes.
Adaptive Performance was created.
Adaptive Performance was destroyed.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.LifecycleEventHandler

summary.para: Adaptive Performance lifecycle events which are sent when the lifecycle of IAdaptivePerformance is changed.

member.param: IAdaptivePerformance instance.
Type of lifecycle change on instance

summary.para: Event arguments for boost changes. These are used in the PerformanceBottleneckChangeHandler.
Is the CPU boosted.
Is the GPU boosted

member.name: T:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.PerformanceBoostChangeHandler

summary.para: You can subscribe to the boost event delegate which sends the PerformanceBoostChangeEventArgs when a boost changes.

member.param: The PerformanceBoostChangeEventArgs that describes the boost event.

summary.para: The performance bottleneck enum describes what is currently limiting the system.
Framerate is limited by CPU processing.
Framerate is limited by GPU processing.
Framerate is limited by Application.targetFrameRate. In this case, you should consider lowering the application's performance requirements (see IDevicePerformanceControl.AutomaticPerformanceControl).
Framerate bottleneck is unknown.
Event arguments for performance bottleneck changes. These are used in the PerformanceBottleneckChangeHandler.
The performance bottleneck reported in the event.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.PerformanceBottleneckChangeHandler

summary.para: You can subscribe to the bottleneck event delegate which sends the PerformanceBottleneckChangeEventArgs when the bottleneck changes.

member.param: The PerformanceBottleneckChangeEventArgs that describes the performance bottleneck state.

summary.para: Enum used to describe the performance control mode used by Adaptive Performance. Can be read from IDevicePerformanceControl.PerformanceControlMode.
Adaptive Performance controls performance levels automatically (default). This mode is enabled by setting IDevicePerformanceControl.AutomaticPerformanceControl to true.

You can control performance levels via IDevicePerformanceControl.CpuLevel and IDevicePerformanceControl.GpuLevel. This mode is enabled by setting IDevicePerformanceControl.AutomaticPerformanceControl to false.

The operating system controls performance levels. This happens if manual control is not supported or if the system is in a thermal throttling state, at which point the operating system takes over control automatically.
Arguments for the performance level change event. These are used in the PerformanceLevelChangeHandler.
The new CPU level.
The difference in CPU levels 0 if the previous or new level equals Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel.
The new GPU level.
The difference in GPU levels. 0 if either the previous or the new level equals Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel.
True if the change was caused by manual adjustments to IDevicePerformanceControl.CpuLevel or IDevicePerformanceControl.GpuLevel during automatic mode, false otherwise.
The current PerformanceControlMode. See IDevicePerformanceControl.PerformanceControlMode.

member.name: T:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.PerformanceLevelChangeHandler

summary.para: You can subscribe to the performance level event delegate which sends the PerformanceLevelChangeEventArgs when the performance level changes.

member.param: The performance level change event is sent to the delegate via the level change event argument.

summary.para: PerformanceMetrics store the current bottleneck, CPU, and GPU levels
Current CPU cluster information information. Updated at application startup.
CPU boosted.

Current CPU performance level. This value updates once per frame when changes are applied to IDevicePerformanceControl.CpuLevel. Value in the range [Constants.MinCpuPerformanceLevel, IDevicePerformanceControl.MaxCpuPerformanceLevel] or Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel.

Current GPU performance level. This value updates once per frame when changes are applied to IDevicePerformanceControl.GpuLevel. Value in the range [Constants.MinGpuPerformanceLevel, IDevicePerformanceControl.MaxGpuPerformanceLevel] or Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel.
GPU boosted.
Current performance bottleneck which describes if the program is CPU, GPU, or Application.targetFrameRate bound.
The performance mode enum describes what is currently the active performance mode of the stystem.
Performance is limited as mode is set to preserve battery.
Performance mode is accelerates CPU.
Performance mode is accelerates GPU.
Performance mode is optimized and may be a mix of accelerating both CPU and GPU.
Default performance mode.
Performance mode is unknown.
You can subscribe to the performance mode event delegate which sends the PerformanceMode when the performance mode changes.
A class to define a provider subsystem for Adaptive Performance.
Lifecycle of the Subsystem.
An abstract class to be implemented by providers of this subsystem.
Returns if the subsystem is running.
Application lifecycle events to be consumed by subsystem. Can be null if the subsystem does not need special handling on life-cycle events. The returned reference does not change after startup.
Bitset of supported features. Does not change after startup.
Returns if the subsystem is initialized successfully.

Control CPU or GPU performance levels of the device. Can be null if the subsystem does not support controlling CPU/GPU performance levels. Is null when the Feature.PerformanceLevelControl bit is not set in Capabilities. The returned reference does not change after startup.
Generates a human readable string of subsystem internal stats. Optional and only used for development.
To be called once per frame. The returned data structure's fields are populated with the latest available data, according to the supported Capabilities.

returns.para: Data structure with the most recent performance data.

summary.para: Returns the version of the subsystem implementation. Can be used together with SubsystemDescriptor to identify a subsystem.
Bitset of supported features. Does not change after startup.
Main constructor, not used in the subsystem specifically.
Returns if the subsystem is initialized successfully.

Control CPU or GPU performance levels of the device. Can be null if the subsystem does not support controlling CPU/GPU performance levels. Is null when the Feature.PerformanceLevelControl bit is not set in Capabilities. The returned reference does not change after startup.
Generates a human readable string of subsystem internal stats. Optional and only used for development.
To be called once per frame. The returned data structure's fields are populated with the latest available data, according to the supported Capabilities.

returns.para: Data structure with the most recent performance data.

summary.para: Returns the version of the subsystem implementation. Can be used together with SubsystemDescriptor to identify a subsystem.
Base class for subsystems to create your custom provider subsystem to deliver data from your provider to Adaptive Performance.
Application lifecycle events to be consumed by subsystem. Can be null if the subsystem does not need special handling on life-cycle events. The returned reference does not change after startup.
Bitset of supported features. Does not change after startup.
Returns if the subsystem is initialized successfully.

Control CPU or GPU performance levels of the device. Can be null if the subsystem does not support controlling CPU/GPU performance levels. Is null when the Feature.PerformanceLevelControl bit is not set in Capabilities. The returned reference does not change after startup.
Generates a human readable string of subsystem internal stats. Optional and only used for development.
To be called once per frame. The returned data structure's fields are populated with the latest available data, according to the supported Capabilities.

returns.para: Data structure with the most recent performance data.

summary.para: Returns the version of the subsystem implementation. Can be used together with SubsystemDescriptor to identify a subsystem.
The Adaptive Performance Subsystem Descriptor is used for describing the subsystem so it can be picked up by the subsystem management system.
Cinfo stores the ID and subsystem implementation type which is used to identify the subsystem during subsystem initialization.
The ID stores the name of the subsystem used to identify it in the subsystem registry.
Specifies the provider implementation type to use for instantiation.
The subsystem implementation type stores the the type used for initialization in the subsystem registry.
Specifies the AdaptivePerformanceSubsystem-derived type that forwards casted calls to its provider.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.Provider.AdaptivePerformanceSubsystemDescriptor.#ctor(UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.Provider.AdaptivePerformanceSubsystemDescriptor/Cinfo)

summary.para: Constructor to fill the subsystem descriptor with all information to register the subsystem successfully.

member.param: Pass in the information about the subsystem.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.Provider.AdaptivePerformanceSubsystemDescriptor.RegisterDescriptor(UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.Provider.AdaptivePerformanceSubsystemDescriptor/Cinfo)

summary.para: Register the subsystem with the subsystem registry and make it available to use during runtime.

member.param: Pass in the information about the subsystem.

returns.para: Returns an active subsystem descriptor.

summary.para: Feature flags See PerformanceDataRecord.ChangeFlags and AdaptivePerformanceSubsystem.Capabilities.
See PerformanceDataRecord.ClusterInfo
See PerformanceDataRecord.CpuFrameTime
See PerformanceDataRecord.CpuPerformanceBoost and IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.EnableCpuBoost
See PerformanceDataRecord.CpuPerformanceLevel and IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.SetPerformanceLevel
See PerformanceDataRecord.GpuFrameTime
See PerformanceDataRecord.GpuPerformanceBoost and IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.EnableGpuBoost
See PerformanceDataRecord.GpuPerformanceLevel and IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.SetPerformanceLevel
No features
See PerformanceDataRecord.OverallFrameTime
See PerformanceDataRecord.PerformanceLevelControlAvailable and AdaptivePerformanceSubsystem.PerformanceLevelControl
See PerformanceDataRecord.PerformanceMode
See PerformanceDataRecord.TemperatureLevel
See PerformanceDataRecord.TemperatureTrend
See PerformanceDataRecord.WarningLevel
This interface describes how the Adaptive Performance provider lifecycle behaves.
Called before an application pauses. To be called from MonoBehaviour.OnApplicationPause.
Called after an application resumes. To be called from MonoBehaviour.OnApplicationPause.
The device performance level control lets you change CPU and GPU levels and informs you about the current levels.
Request a CPU performance boost.

returns.para: Returns true on success. When this fails, it means that the system took control and does not allow boosts.

summary.para: Request a GPU performance boost.

returns.para: Returns true on success. When this fails, it means that the system took control and does not allow boosts.

summary.para: Maximum supported CPU performance level. This should not change after startup. Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel in case performance levels are not supported. Value in the range of [Constants.MinCpuPerformanceLevel, 10].

Maximum supported GPU performance level. This should not change after startup. Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel in case performance levels are not supported. Value in the range of [Constants.MinGpuPerformanceLevel, 10].

member.name: M:UnityEngine.AdaptivePerformance.Provider.IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.SetPerformanceLevel(System.Int32&,System.Int32&)

summary.para: Request a performance level change. If Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel is passed, the subsystem picks the level to be used.

member.param: The new performance level. Can be Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel or range of [Constants.MinCpuPerformanceLevel, IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.MaxCpuPerformanceLevel]. If Feature.CpuPerformanceLevel is not supported (see AdaptivePerformanceSubsystem.Capabilities), this parameter is ignored.

The new performance level. Can be Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel or range of [Constants.MinCpuPerformanceLevel, IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.MaxGpuPerformanceLevel]. If Feature.GpuPerformanceLevel is not supported (see AdaptivePerformanceSubsystem.Capabilities), this parameter is ignored.

returns.para: Returns true on success. When this fails, it means that the system took control of the active performance levels.

summary.para: The performance data record stores all information about the thermal and performance status and delivers it from the provider subsystem to Adaptive Performance for further processing.

A bitset of features which indicate if their value changed in the last frame or at startup. Unsupported features will never change. Fields not changing always have valid data as long as its capability is supported.
Current CPU cluster information information. Includes number of big, medium and small cores use at the application startup.
The time in seconds spent by the CPU for rendering the last complete frame. Has changed when Feature.CpuFrameTime bit is set in ChangeFlags.

The currently active CPU boost state. This is typically true if previously enabled with IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.EnableCpuBoost once the boost is successfully applied. Adaptive Performance might also change this level on its own. This typically happens when the device is thermal throttling or when IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.EnableCpuBoost fails. Once the CPU boost is enabled it is active until you receive a callback that it is disabled. CPU boost level has changed when Feature.CpuPerformanceBoost bit is set in ChangeFlags.

The currently active CPU performance level. This is typically the value previously set with IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.SetPerformanceLevel once the levels are successfully applied. Adaptive Performance might also change this level on its own. This typically happens when the device is thermal throttling or when IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.SetPerformanceLevel failed. CPU performance level has a value in the range of [Constants.MinCpuPerformanceLevel, IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.MaxCpuPerformanceLevel], or Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel. A value of Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel means that Adaptive Performance took control of performance levels. CPU performance level has changed when Feature.CpuPerformanceLevel bit is set in ChangeFlags.
The time in seconds spent by the GPU for rendering the last complete frame. Has changed when Feature.GpuFrameTime bit is set in ChangeFlags.

The currently active GPU boost state. This is typically true if previously enabled with IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.EnableGpuBoost once the boost is successfully applied. Adaptive Performance might also change this level on its own. This typically happens when the device is thermal throttling or when IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.EnableGpuBoost fails. Once the GPU boost is enabled it is active until you receive a callback that it is disabled. GPU boost level has changed when Feature.GpuPerformanceBoost bit is set in ChangeFlags.

The currently active GPU performance level. This is typically the value previously set with IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.SetPerformanceLevel once the levels are successfully applied. Adaptive Performance might also change this level on its own. This typically happens when the device is thermal throttling or when IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.SetPerformanceLevel failed. GPU performance level has a value in the range of [Constants.MinCpuPerformanceLevel, IDevicePerformanceLevelControl.MaxGpuPerformanceLevel], or Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel. A value of Constants.UnknownPerformanceLevel means that Adaptive Performance took control of performance levels. GPU performance level has changed when Feature.GpuPerformanceLevel bit is set in ChangeFlags.
The total time in seconds spent for the frame. Has changed when Feature.OverallFrameTime bit is set in ChangeFlags.

True if =performance levels can currently be controlled manually and aren't controlled by Adaptive Performance or the operating system. Has changed when Feature.PerformanceLevelControl bit is set in ChangeFlags.
Current Performance mode information.

The current normalized temperature level in the range of [0.0, 1.0], or -1.0 when not supported or not available right now. A level of 1.0 means that the device is thermal throttling. The temperature level has changed when the Feature.TemperatureLevel bit is set in ChangeFlags.

The current temperature trend in the range of [-1.0, 1.0] that is a metric of temperature change over time. The temperature trend is constant at 0.0 in case the feature is not supported. The temperature trend has changed when Feature.TemperatureTrend bit is set in ChangeFlags.
The current warning level as documented in Feature.WarningLevel. The warning level has changed when Feature.WarningLevel bit is set in ChangeFlags.
Bottleneck flags that the scaler targets.
The scaler targets the CPU and attempts to reduce the CPU load.
The scaler targets fillrate, often at the expense of visual quality.
The scaler targets the GPU and attempts to reduce the GPU load.
Scaler impact on visuals.
High impact on visual quality. The scaler will have an easily visible effect on quality.
Low impact on visual quality. Changes might not be very noticeable to the user.
Medium impact on visual quality. Mildly affects the visuals in the scene and might be noticeable to the user.
Describes what action is needed to stabilize.
Quality must be decreased.
Quality must be decreased as soon as possible.
Recommended to increase quality.
No action is required.
You can subscribe to the thermal event delegate which sends the ThermalMetrics when the thermal state changes.
ThermalMetrics stores the thermal state as TemperatureLevel, TemperatureTrend, and WarningLevel.

Current normalized temperature level in the range of [0, 1]. A value of 0 means standard operation temperature and that the device is not in a throttling state. A value of 1 means that the device has reached maximum temperature and is either going into or is already in throttling state.

Current normalized temperature trend in the range of [-1, 1]. A value of 1 describes a rapid increase in temperature. A value of 0 describes a constant temperature. A value of -1 describes a rapid decrease in temperature. Note: It takes at least 10s until the temperature trend can start reflecting any changes.
Current thermal warning level.
Warning levels are used in the ThermalMetrics and describe the thermal status of the device. There are three possible statuses.
No warning is the normal warning level during standard thermal state.
If the application is in the throttling state, it should make adjustments to go back to normal temperature levels.
If throttling is imminent, the application should perform adjustments to avoid thermal throttling.
Adaptive Performance is a built-in module that allows users to adjust settings based on performance metrics.
